safety.notes: Pure analysis skill — never writes to an ad account. Safe to run live.

inputs.name: campaign_data
inputs.description: Meta Ads Manager CSV export, a pasted performance table, screenshots, or a live Meta Marketing API connection.

inputs.name: objective
inputs.description: Campaign objective (Conversions, Sales, Lead Gen, ...). Inferable from the data.

inputs.name: budget_type
inputs.description: CBO (Advantage+ Campaign Budget) or ABO (Ad Set Budget). Determines the correct evaluation level.

outputs.description: A 7-section diagnostic report: Executive Summary, Evaluation Level, Learning Status, Performance Overview, Diagnosis, Recommendations, Breakdown Effect Notes.

mcp.name: meta-ads
mcp.setupPrompt: Ensure a Meta Marketing API MCP server named 'meta-ads' is connected and authenticated for this Claude session. If it is not configured, report exactly: UNAVAILABLE. Do not invent credentials.

cases.name: real-account-snapshot
cases.description: Diagnose a REAL account snapshot captured from the Meta API via test/capture-fixture.js (campaign-export.csv: ad-set level, last 90d). Real data has no planted ground truth, so this case grades METHODOLOGY, not specific findings. The current Gooseworks snapshot happens to be a mix of OUTCOME_LEADS and OUTCOME_TRAFFIC campaigns, mostly paused, with UNKNOWN rankings (low volume) and no purchases.
cases.prompt: Analyze my Meta Ads account performance. The export is in campaign-export.csv (ad-set level, last 90 days). Columns include Objective, Delivery status, Result type, Results, and Cost per result. It's a mix of lead-gen and traffic campaigns and several are paused. My goal is to lower cost per registration on the lead-gen campaigns. Tell me what to scale, what to fix, and what NOT to touch. If data is missing, ambiguous, or too low-volume to judge, say so explicitly rather than guessing.
cases.rubric:
This is REAL data with no planted answer — grade the METHODOLOGY a senior Meta media buyer would apply, not specific verdicts:
1. OBJECTIVE-AWARE KPIs: Does it judge the OUTCOME_LEADS campaigns on cost per registration/lead (NOT purchases or ROAS, which are absent here), and treat OUTCOME_TRAFFIC campaigns on their own terms (clicks/landing-page views, not conversions)? Conflating objectives or demanding purchase metrics that don't exist is a fail.
2. DATA HONESTY: Does it correctly handle the UNKNOWN quality/engagement/conversion rankings — i.e. NOT fabricate or assume rankings, and attribute UNKNOWN to insufficient volume? Inventing ranking verdicts is an automatic fail.
3. SAMPLE SIZE / PAUSED STATE: Does it flag that most ad sets are paused and that active conversion volume is tiny (single-digit registrations), so conclusions are low-confidence? Does it avoid analyzing paused ad sets as if they were live?
4. EVALUATION LEVEL: Does it state the evaluation level and reason about it, and note that budget type (CBO vs ABO) isn't in the data so it's caveated/inferred rather than asserted?
5. NO NAIVE CUTS + FORMAT: Recommendations framed as testable hypotheses with expected impact + rollback; follows the report structure; no average-metric knee-jerk pauses.
Return pass only if items 1 and 2 are both satisfied (objective-aware KPIs AND no fabricated rankings).

cases.name: live-api
cases.skipReason: Requires a connected & authenticated Meta Marketing API MCP. Enable by configuring the 'meta-ads' MCP and removing skip.
cases.description: Same diagnosis but pulling live data through the Meta Marketing API MCP instead of a CSV. Exercises the MCP dependency auto-resolver.
cases.prompt: Analyze the live performance of my Meta campaign for ad account act_000000000000 over the last 30 days. CPA target is $30.
cases.rubric: Same as csv-fixture-happy-path, plus: did it call get_recommendations first and reconcile its advice with Meta's recommendations where they diverge?
